title: Charity MCC Micro-Donation Card Validation with Escalation Pattern
narrative: Fraudsters exploit charitable organizations (MCC 8398, 8641) with their historically lower fraud controls and reluctance to dispute small donations. Attack begins with 8-15 micro-donations of $1.00-$3.00 across multiple charity merchants within 45-90 minutes, testing card validity with minimal risk of immediate blocks. Successful cards show immediate escalation: within 2-6 hours, the same card attempts $200-$800 purchases at high-risk MCCs (5735 music stores, 5815 digital goods, 5941 sporting goods). The charity transactions cluster in late-night hours (00:00-05:00 UTC) when fraud teams have reduced coverage. Email domains are temporary services (mailinator.com, guerrillamail.com) with card1_txn_count_1day jumping from 0 to 12-18. Distance between billing (dist1) and merchant shows 800+ miles, while P_emaildomain and R_emaildomain mismatch in 95% of cases.
indicators: 8-15 transactions to MCC 8398/8641 merchants with TransactionAmt between $1.00-$3.00 within 45-90 minute window
card1_txn_count_1day increases from 0 to 12+ with charity MCCs comprising first 8-15 transactions chronologically
Escalation pattern: $200-$800 purchases at MCC 5735/5815/5941 within 2-6 hours after initial charity donations
85%+ of charity test transactions occur during txn_hour 0-5 (00:00-05:00 UTC)
P_emaildomain matches temporary email services (mailinator, guerrillamail, tempmail) in 90%+ of transactions
dist1 exceeds 800 miles between billing address and charity merchant location
card1_amt_zscore spikes from <0.5 during testing phase to >3.5 during exploitation phase
summary: Multiple small donations ($1-$3) to charities within 90 minutes, followed within hours by large purchases ($200-$800) at digital goods or sporting goods merchants using the same card. Charity transactions cluster in overnight hours with temporary email addresses and distant billing locations.
reasoning: Criminals use charity donations as low-risk card validation because nonprofits rarely flag small gifts, then immediately exploit confirmed-valid cards for high-value fraudulent purchases before the cardholder notices unauthorized activity.
indicator_explanations: Card makes rapid-fire micro-donations to charities to test if it works
Card goes from dormant to suddenly active with charity donations leading the burst
After donation tests succeed, card pivots to expensive electronics or clothing purchases
Testing donations happen in the dead of night when victims are asleep
Email address comes from temporary disposable email services used once and discarded
Billing address is hundreds of miles away from the charity merchant locations
Spending amount jumps from tiny test amounts to massively abnormal high-value purchases
